Overview

Address
220 EAST 24TH STREET, NATIONAL CITY, CA 91950
Phone
6194746741
Certified beds
98
Avg daily residents
89 (91% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1968-08-01
Setting
Urban
4 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 4/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 3/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.93 4.00 3.79 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.59 0.70 0.57 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.74 0.89 0.71 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.60 2.41 2.51 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.33 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.15 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.73
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.54
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.60
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
36.9%
annual
RN turnover
43.8%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.42
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.03
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2022-09-22 14 6 8 80 1 80
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2019-10-11 12 9 3 76 1 76
Total weighted health score
79.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (39)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

37 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
2 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-12-11 2025-12-18
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-12-11 2025-12-18
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-07-02 —
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. D 2025-07-02 —
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2025-05-13 2025-05-27
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-05-13 2025-05-27
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. D 2025-05-13 2025-05-27
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-04-14 2025-04-25
0622 Not transfer or discharge a resident without an adequate reason; and must provide documentation and convey specific information when a resident is transferred or discharged. D 2024-12-23 2025-01-14
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2024-08-22 2024-09-20
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-08-22 2024-09-20
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. E 2022-09-22 2022-10-22
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2022-09-22 2022-10-22
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2022-09-22 2022-10-22
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2022-09-22 2022-10-22
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2022-09-22 2022-10-22
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2022-09-22 2022-10-22
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2019-10-11 2019-11-01
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2019-10-11 2019-11-01
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2019-10-11 2019-11-01
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2019-10-11 2019-11-01
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2019-10-11 2019-11-01
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2019-10-11 2019-11-01
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. E 2019-10-11 2019-11-01
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. E 2019-10-11 2019-11-01
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2019-10-11 2019-11-01
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2018-10-04 2018-11-17
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2018-10-04 2018-11-17
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2018-10-04 2018-11-17
0660 Plan the resident's discharge to meet the resident's goals and needs. D 2018-10-04 2018-11-17
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2018-10-04 2018-11-17
0687 Provide appropriate foot care. D 2018-10-04 2018-11-17
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2018-10-04 2018-11-17
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2018-10-04 2018-11-17
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2018-10-04 2018-11-17
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2018-10-04 2018-11-17
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2018-10-04 2018-11-17
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. D 2018-10-04 2018-11-17
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. E 2018-10-04 2018-11-17
